title: Global Search Entry Point — Persistent Ctrl+K Command Palette
description: The application shall provide a persistent global search field accessible from the primary application navigation. The recommended demo shortcut is Ctrl+K / Cmd+K.

title: Cross-Application Search Scope — Employees, Plans, Requirements, and More
description: The global search shall search across key entity types. The architecture shall allow additional entity types to be added without redesigning the search experience.

title: Employee Search — By Name, ID, Email, or Employer Group
description: Users with appropriate permissions shall be able to search employees by first name, last name, full name, employee ID, email, SSN last four digits, or employer group. Employee results navigate to Employee 360.

title: Employee ID Exact Lookup — Returns Employee and Related Records
description: The system shall support exact employee ID lookup returning the employee record and key related entities (enrollment, COBRA status).

title: Plan Search — By Name, ID, Type, Carrier, or Plan Year
description: Users shall be able to search plans by name, plan ID, plan type, carrier, and plan year. Results show plan status and effective dates.

title: Requirement Search — By ID, Keyword, Title, Epic, Status, or Priority
description: Users shall be able to search requirements by ID, keyword, title, description, epic, feature, status, and priority. Clicking a result opens the Requirements Studio.

title: Audit Search — By Actor, Entity, Action, Date Range, or Change Type
description: Authorized users (Auditor, Benefits Admin, System Admin) shall be able to search audit records by actor, entity type, entity ID, action, date range, and change type.

title: Carrier Transaction Search — By Transaction ID, Employee, Carrier, Status, or Date
description: Users shall be able to search carrier transactions by transaction ID, employee ID, employee name, carrier, plan, transaction type, status, and date.

title: Search Results Grouping — Results Organized by Entity Type
description: Search results shall be grouped by entity type. Each group shows a count and a preview of results. This is significantly more useful than a single flat result list.

title: Search Result Preview — Quick-Look Panel on Hover or Selection
description: Hovering over or selecting a search result shall display a quick preview with key fields and a deep-link action button. This reduces unnecessary navigation to open records.

title: Search Filters — Refine Results by Type, Status, Date, and Entity-Specific Criteria
description: Users shall be able to refine search results using generic filters (entity type, status, date, employer, plan year) and dynamically displayed entity-specific filters.

title: Search Result Ranking — Exact Match First, Then Prefix, Then Keyword
description: Search results shall be ranked by match quality: exact ID match, exact name match, exact phrase, prefix match, keyword match.

title: Role-Based Search Results — RBAC Applied to All Entity Types
description: Search results shall respect the user's RBAC permissions. An employee searching for another employee's name shall receive no unauthorized results.

title: Search Result Deep Linking — Direct Navigation to Target Screen
description: Each search result shall navigate directly to the relevant screen based on entity type.

title: Search Empty State — Helpful Suggestions When No Results Found
description: When no results are found, the system shall display a helpful empty state with search suggestions.

title: Search Loading Experience — Per-Entity-Type Loading Indicators
description: Search shall provide a responsive loading state with per-entity-type loading indicators. For the demo, results should appear within 1–2 seconds for local seeded data.
performanceTarget: Results appear within 1–2 seconds for local seeded data

title: AI Natural Language Search — Intent Interpretation Across Entity Types
description: The system may support natural language queries that are interpreted by the AI Benefits Assistant and converted to structured filters. The AI agentic search pattern is a compelling P2 demo capability.

exampleInteractions.query: Show me employees enrolled in PPO 500 who have pending carrier transactions.
Find all COBRA cases expiring this week.

title: Unified Search API Response Schema — Consistent Entity-Agnostic Result Shape
description: The search API shall return a standardized response shape regardless of entity type, enabling the UI to render results consistently.
architectureNote: For demo, simple in-memory indexed search or server-side filtering across JSON collections is sufficient. API contract should remain unchanged when the backend moves from LowDB to a real database.
